warnings: No warnings array was present in the result JSON (empty). The integrity block reported three checks, all ok=true: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ — "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"; fukui_charge_conservation_f- — "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"; schema_header — "common header present".

prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of furan** — DFT, tier `standard` overridden to explicit **B3LYP / def2-TZVP** (PySCF, RKS; exact four-center integrals — density fitting OFF by default), **gas phase** (no implicit solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1. The finite-difference Fukui functions are built from three separate SCF calculations: neutral (N, charge 0, mult 1), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2), with condensed Fukui indices derived from **Mulliken (PySCF) partial charges**.

Geometry provenance: the user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz` (9 atoms: O, 4×C, 4×H) was used **as-is** — this Fukui workflow performs single-point SCFs at the given geometry; the structure was not re-optimized here.

Software: PySCF (via the chemkit `fukui` engine subcommand).

**Headline result — most electrophilic site (largest f⁺, nucleophilic-attack susceptibility):** atom index 5 (1-based), a **carbon**, with **f⁺ = 1.9243** (dimensionless condensed Fukui index). The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻) is the same carbon (atom 5) with f⁻ = 0.16164.

Per-atom results (order: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H):
- f⁺: [−1.33162, 1.22869, 1.22703, 1.92331, 1.92429, −1.21906, −1.21911, −0.76678, −0.76674]
- f⁻: [0.083822, 0.089103, 0.089305, 0.16153, 0.16164, 0.10248, 0.10251, 0.10482, 0.10479]
- f⁰ (average): [−0.62390, 0.65889, 0.65817, 1.04242, 1.04297, −0.55829, −0.55830, −0.33098, −0.33097]
- dual descriptor (f⁺−f⁻): [−1.41544, 1.13958, 1.13772, 1.76178, 1.76266, −1.32155, −1.32162, −0.87160, −0.87153]

Note that several f⁺ and dual-descriptor values are negative/large in magnitude, which is common when condensed Fukui indices are computed from Mulliken populations (they are basis-set-sensitive and not guaranteed to be strictly positive or bounded); interpret the *relative* ranking of sites rather than the absolute magnitudes.

**Convergence / integrity:** the engine reports integrity status = **ok, trustworthy = true**. All three integrity checks passed (ok=true): the f⁺ and f⁻ condensed indices each conserve to sum = 1.0000 (a well-behaved result — large drift would indicate an unconverged N±1 state), and the schema header is present. No `warnings` array was present in the result JSON (i.e. no engine warnings). 

**Reproducibility.** Command: `.../cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults --stdout json <furan.xyz> --out .../live.json`. (`--accept-defaults` was passed only to consent to the gas-phase default; the functional and basis were set explicitly.) The live `.out` log is streamed by the engine to the run directory during execution and can be `tail -f`'d; the result JSON and the per-atom bar chart PNG were written under `.../20260704-053627_furan_fukui_reactivity/`.
